meta.title: aish — the AI-native shell
meta.description: aish is an open-source, provider-agnostic AI shell for developers. v0.5 makes the binary self-managing: aish update pulls the latest release and aish uninstall removes it cleanly — aish commit still built in, zero setup. Free software, MIT-licensed.

hero.badge: v0.5 shipped — aish update keeps the binary current

subhead.text: is an open-source, provider-agnostic AI shell for developers.
is built in — it turns your staged diff into a commit message you can accept, edit, or reject, with no install step beyond the binary itself.
removes the binary (and optionally its data) cleanly.

hero.installCaption: Linux & macOS · x86_64 / arm64 · or build from source with cargo.

features.heading: What aish is designed to be

items.title: Commit messages, built in
items.body: aish commit reads your staged diff and proposes a Conventional Commits message; accept, edit in $EDITOR, or reject. Identical requests hit a deterministic local cache, every generation lands in an audit log, and aish usage turns that log into token and cost reports.

items.title: Provider-agnostic
items.body: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Gemini, Ollama, and Kilo behind one config and model alias. Never locked to a single vendor — swap models with a flag.

items.title: Open & inspectable
items.body: MIT-licensed free software, shipped as a single static Rust binary. Read the specs, audit the design, send a patch — built in the open from day one.
